We have just, like, a different aspect of that.
We have primarily a self-serve audience that we're shifting toward.
And there are people who are interested in adopting AI, but, you know, aren't, like, deep in that space.
And so it's a matter of, like, adjusting that kind of content toward someone who is not as familiar.
But, like, we're not trying to dumb it down for them too much, right?
Like, make it more approachable.
So that's good to hear.
Speaking of AI, so there's been this massive shift, not just in terms of, like, how we're creating the content, but in, like, the topical focus of the content.
And I've seen it on the mail blog, you know, like, explainers about AI agents, et cetera.
So, like, what level of expertise does the team have with these topics and how do you stay on top of trends in the space to inform content strategy?
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, all of us are pretty tapped into AI and what's happening in the space.
Like, definitely have ongoing, even just, like, internal, internal sort of, like, webinars of sorts where we're all just sort of learning from each other.
And we actually have this, like, new...
It's like a separate product, actually, but it's called like the AI-led growth community.
And like, that's where we're always just sort of like talking about like what's happening in the space.
And we're actually also then offering these webinars to clients to actually kind of get them all up to speed on what's happening in GEO specifically, but then AI just more broadly.
So we're all have a decent amount of expertise, some admittedly, like more than others, but there's collectively, we have a very, very high expertise in the space.
That's great.
Leah, do you have more questions you want to ask?
Yes, I have one more question.
Well, two, maybe if we have time, something just more process oriented.
And now for just a standard mid-length, fairly non-technical article, what does your typical production timeline look like from brief to final draft?
And what factors influence how quickly you guys can turn content around?
I know you already touched on our reviews sort of add more time to that, but is there anything else?
Yeah, I mean, so typically, like, in, before we would get started, it's like we, I think right now, and Tyler, correct me if I'm wrong, but we're tending to offer five editorial articles per week.
But, you know, that can also be, you know, discussed.
Sorry, Tyler, I don't want to speak.
No, no, no, you're good.
Yeah, every new client, we do five a week, but with Superhuman, we talked about doing 10 a week because there's a combo.
So of NetNew and Refresh, and also because you guys have a big need on the scale side.
So 10 a week would be the world with you.
Okay.
And then, yeah, and then in terms of actual process, so we have our content team sort of working throughout the week, kind of like chipping away at like those 10 articles.
And then via Slack, we would update you when blog posts are ready for review.
So it's kind of just like on a rolling basis.
And then the idea is that by the end of the week, all of those 10 have been published.
You're muted, Tyler.
Okay.
I'm not going to do that again.
Like in the Superhuman Slack channel right now, Joe from our team, 12 refreshes that they're ready to publish and like about 10 articles in production for the next week.
And that is is And
With no reviews, no editing or anything like that, that's adding to that, right?
So if we did want to review each article, do you think we could still do that same commitment or how much time would that add?
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Yeah, it's just, and that's where we would just sort of work together to determine what the best review cadence is.

Like, sometimes they say, like, okay, like, do you make sure that you get me at least, like, four articles to review by Wednesday, and then we can get them those four, and then we're, like, working on the rest, and then we get them next to them, like, maybe by Friday morning or Thursday morning, just sort of depending.
So, yeah, it's just a matter of making sure that we are aligned on actual, like, timing and, like, for those actual reviews.
Because, like, obviously, like, we don't want to get to a Friday and no content has been reviewed.
Like, that's not, that wouldn't be ideal.
But, yeah, just making sure it's, like, those.
Reviews are happening on a rolling basis.

Yeah, because we definitely have gotten into a place where some clients have wanted to review the content, but then they end up having a backlog of 25 pieces, and we're like, okay, well, we haven't been able to publish anything.
And so it's, yeah, just making sure that we can all be on track with that.
Yeah, I think if we understand your review cadence, your review process, everything that we send to you has already been reviewed by our team.
And we also set up this content OS so that it's not like these articles are coming out of nowhere, right?
We've got like a proof to start considering a backlog of assignments.
They all come from those topic clusters that we agree on in the first part of the engagement.
And then for everything that we would send your way, it would be in a Google Doc for easy reviews.
And once you give us a thumbs up, that's when we can publish.
But we're already working on the following week's articles as you're going through your reviews.
Cool.
That makes sense.
And I know we're just about at time, but last question from me.
We produce a lot of content that's a part of a series.
So everything's sort of like topically similar, but a little bit different.

I find that that can sometimes be difficult for AI when it's purely written by AI to not have too much overlap between the articles to make sure that we're still using like consistent definitions throughout.
How does your team typically handle producing content in a series like that?

Yeah, so we would actually probably have a new, have a specific pipeline for that, that series, just so that everything, everything is living in that same project space so that it already has the context of what it already wrote.
Because, yeah.
You're right.
I have definitely seen that, too.
And then you kind of end up coming up with, like, okay, well, these two articles, like, feel, like, very similar.
But I think, yeah, that is using our agentive workflows is how we would go about doing that.

But again, also the human in the loop on the end, making sure that, like, because ultimately it's still going to be the same human looking at all the content in that series and then making sure that it actually is distinct from the last.
From the last in the batch.
Think about it, too, as, like, stages in the process, right?
The first stage in the process, we're just going to create super high-quality SEO and GEO-optimized content that addresses keyword and competitive gaps.
And then a series, like, we would recommend if we're looking to drive results, let's do that later, right?
Like, once we've established that the SEO GEO content is performing and there's a need for us to execute.
Thank you.
Thank
on the series.
And then for the series to Megan's point, we can just set up a different pipeline so that it's not using the same pipeline that we use for the initial content that we focus on.

Yeah, but yeah, if y'all have other questions, like you can also just like Slack me directly or email me, like happy to answer any other questions that like might come up after this, just about like our general process.
Cause I know it's, it can, it can be a lot.
So thank you, yeah, I know we're over time and this has been super helpful just to get more of a peek under the hood and hear from someone who's going end to end with all the content.
So really appreciate your time, Megan and Tyler, thanks for organizing.
Yeah, definitely Jess.
I know you mentioned like trying to think through the decision this week.
So, uh, happy to help out over Slack or.

Jumping on another call, I know we didn't talk too much about programmatic today, but we do feel confident executing on those use cases, and I've done it before, so happy to chat more about that as well.
Great.
Well, yeah, we're going to debrief, and I will be in touch.
Awesome.
All right, thanks so much, everyone.
Thank you.
bye, have a good one.
See you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Deep dive into GrowthX's content creation and review processes.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Human-in-the-Loop Process:** GrowthX's "Atlas" platform uses mandatory human review at key stages (brief, outline, draft) to ensure quality, accuracy, and brand voice.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=803.0)
  - [**Fact-Checking Protocol:** For technical content, the AI fact-checks against client-specified sources (e.g., official government sites), and a human editor then manually verifies all links and information.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
  - [**High-Volume Production:** The proposed plan is 10 articles/week (net new + refreshes) for Superhuman, double the standard 5/week, to meet the brand's rapid scaling needs.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2428.0)
  - [**Strategic Partnership:** A 2-phase model starts with an 8-week strategy sprint to build trust and calibrate quality before committing to a 12-month partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1148.0)

## Topics

### Superhuman's Content Challenge

  - [**Strategic Goal:** Broaden Superhuman's website focus and audience as part of a major rebrand.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=639.0)
  - [**Key Constraint:** The Grammarly website will be maintained due to its high search equity; the Coda site is under evaluation.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=726.0)
  - [**Primary Need:** Rapidly scale high-quality content for the main Superhuman site.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2452.0)

### GrowthX's Solution: The Atlas Workflow

  - [**Function:** An end-to-end partner for SEO/AEO content, acting as an extension of the client's team.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)
  - [**Method:** Combines sophisticated AI workflows in the "Atlas" platform with expert human oversight.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [**Core Principle:** Content quality is directly tied to the quality of the inputs ("artifacts") provided by the client.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1095.0)

### Human-in-the-Loop Review Process

  - [**Mandatory Human Touchpoints:**](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=891.0)
      - [**Brief:** Editor validates the AI-generated assignment brief.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=891.0)
      - [**Outline:** Editor reviews and edits the AI-generated outline.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=926.0)
      - [**Draft:** Editor performs a deep read and final review.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=961.0)
  - [**Goal:** Keep final human editing time under 30 minutes per article. If it exceeds this, it signals that the initial inputs (artifacts) need refinement.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1045.0)

### Fact-Checking for Technical Accuracy

  - [**AI Fact-Checker:** A workflow step that validates content against client-specified, authoritative sources.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1388.0)
  - [**Human Verification:** An editor manually clicks all links and verifies information to prevent errors, especially for highly technical or compliance-related topics.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
  - [**Product Accuracy:** The initial strategy sprint builds a "company context" artifact with product details. This artifact is the source of truth, reducing the need for client review over time as trust is established.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1566.0)

### SEO & AEO Integration

  - [**SEO (Traditional Search):**](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1787.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Keyword gap analysis (SEMrush) to find opportunities where competitors rank.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=557.0)
      - [**Performance:** Weekly/bi-weekly meetings to review metrics (clicks, impressions, conversions) and pivot strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1828.0)
      - [**Optimization:** Content refreshes (meta titles, content updates) to improve click-through rates.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1980.0)
  - [**AEO/GEO (AI Search):**](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1787.0)
      - [**Tracking:** Uses Scrunch and LLM referrals in GA4 to monitor AI visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=1856.0)
      - [**Execution:** Workflows are engineered with GEO best practices (e.g., structured data, clear definitions) to create content favored by LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2061.0)

### Production Timeline & Logistics

  - [**Production Volume:** 10 articles/week for Superhuman (5 net new, 5 refreshes).](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2428.0)
  - [**Workflow:** Content is delivered for review on a rolling basis throughout the week via Google Docs.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2469.0)
  - [**Review Cadence:** A clear, agreed-upon review schedule is critical to prevent publishing bottlenecks.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2526.0)
  - [**Content Series:** A dedicated workflow pipeline is used to maintain context and ensure consistency while preventing overlap between articles in a series.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2646.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Superhuman Team:** Debrief internally and decide on the partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2813.0)
  - [**Jess Mordo:** Contact Tyler Pavlas with the decision or for more information, including on programmatic content.](https://fathom.video/share/YHP7ivQWhJfwE1fQjWVfREujWopiqvoo?tab=summary&timestamp=2813.0)

action_items.description: Debrief w/ Leah & Mark; then Slack/email Tyler re: decision + review cadence
